title: Bind Quick Install to verified release archive evidence
rawUserRequest: 开始实施 Conditional GO 评审整改计划的全部工单，严格按序完成每个工单的实现、测试、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步，全部完成后发布新版本。

declaredIntent.summary: Make Quick Install consume and verify immutable release archive evidence before invoking the installer.

outOfScope: Bootstrap lifecycle schema and Wizard implementation
Ownership Manifest and full Installed Lifecycle redesign
Independent CI/Release Evidence Work Item beyond the archive asset binding required here
Publishing a new release; final publication remains the plan-level final step

sources.reason: Quick Install currently verifies an archive only when an environment override is supplied.
Published release contract is the authoritative metadata source for Quick Install.
Public release checker currently validates evidence assets but not a source archive asset.
Release workflow creates evidence assets and must publish the exact source archive.
Quick Install shell contract regression tests.
Public release identity and evidence verification regression tests.
Release and Quick Install evidence boundary documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: The change adds deterministic release-evidence validation and a fail-closed installer gate; it does not publish, mutate, or operate on a production system.

executionDecision.reason: The user authorized serial implementation of all Conditional GO remediation Work Items, including release metadata, workflow, tests, and documentation.

preReviewWarnings: Review that Quick Install consumes only published release.json, verifies the exact tag commit and installer digest, downloads the declared archive asset, and fails closed on every missing or mismatched field.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record checkpoints before editing and before finish to reduce release-boundary drift.

acceptance: release.json declares releaseTag, sourceCommit, installerDigest, and releaseArchive.assetName/sourceCommit/sha256/url for the published release contract.

Anonymous Quick Install defaults to release.json and verifies tag commit, installer digest, downloaded release archive asset name, archive SHA256, and archive sourceCommit before invoking the installer.
Missing metadata, candidate metadata, mismatched tag/source/installer/archive, unavailable asset, and altered asset all fail closed with deterministic diagnostics.
The release workflow publishes the exact source archive asset and verifies its digest and source identity before Draft Release publication.
Public release distribution checks independently validate the declared archive asset and capability status; local/configured source paths remain explicitly non-public verification paths.
Regression tests cover positive verification and every required fail-closed mismatch class, and Quick Install/release documentation describes the evidence boundary.
guidelines: The default Quick Install path must consume only published release.json metadata and must not consult next-release.json.
Release claims must be supported by independently verifiable tag, commit, digest, and asset evidence.
Missing, stale, or mismatched release evidence must fail closed rather than silently downgrade verification.

intent.problem: Quick Install currently reads a default release tag but only validates a source archive when AI_COCKPIT_TEMPLATE_SHA256 is manually supplied; release metadata and the workflow do not bind a published source archive asset to the tag commit and installer digest.
constraints: Use release.json as the only default public metadata source; never consult next-release.json for Quick Install.
Fail closed on missing digest, tag mismatch, source commit mismatch, installer digest mismatch, archive mismatch, or missing release asset.
Keep local/configured source flows explicit and do not let them masquerade as anonymous public verification.
Do not claim enterprise security, signatures, or Calibration completion from archive verification alone.
intent.rationale: A fixed tag is not sufficient evidence that the installer bytes and source archive are the intended release; binding all artifacts to one immutable source commit makes the Quick Install claim independently checkable.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Anonymous Quick Install verifies a matching tag, source commit, installer digest, archive asset name, archive SHA256, and archive source commit before invoking installation.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The low-risk Contract records the behavior boundary before implementation; clean-environment execution is required at finish and is mapped to acceptance evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing or mismatched release metadata, tag, installer, archive, or public asset fails closed without invoking the installer.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The negative path is a finish-stage regression scenario, not a preflight decision.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized the complete serial Conditional GO remediation plan, including release metadata and workflow changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore the prior Quick Install/release evidence behavior.
